negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: No audited repo implements ScheduledTxService event-processing channels whose capacity is parsed from RITUAL_SCHEDULER_CHANNEL_SIZE with a fallback of 1000 when absent or invalid.
related_but_insufficient_code: The v2 port has unrelated transaction-pool/network/RPC mpsc channels, but no scheduler event-processing service or RITUAL_SCHEDULER_CHANNEL_SIZE parser. Nodebuilder docs mention the deprecated old ScheduledTxService location but do not implement it.

notes: Confirmed gap: the old scheduling service surface is absent, and no replacement env-var-controlled scheduler event channel was found.
rationale: This candidate is a standalone operational invariant for scheduler event-processing capacity and is not superseded by later bounded candidates.
statement: ScheduledTxService event-processing channels must use capacity parsed from RITUAL_SCHEDULER_CHANNEL_SIZE, falling back to 1000 when the variable is absent or invalid.
suggested_falsification_tests: Instantiate ScheduledTxService with RITUAL_SCHEDULER_CHANNEL_SIZE unset, invalid, and set to a valid small value, and assert the constructed mpsc channels use 1000, 1000, and the configured value respectively.
Restart event processing after changing the variable and assert recreated channels use the helper's parsed capacity.
summary: Scheduler event-processing channel capacity via RITUAL_SCHEDULER_CHANNEL_SIZE is absent from the audited v2 port and dependency repos.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set RITUAL_SCHEDULER_CHANNEL_SIZE to unset, invalid, and a small valid value, then instantiate the current scheduled event-processing service; no such service/channel construction exists in the audited repo set, so the test cannot be written without restoring that surface.
title: Scheduler channel size is configurable
